verses.text: Raise no false report. Put not thy hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness. Follow not a multitude to do evil; speak not in a cause to wrest judgment. Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause. Guidance: Fairness goes both ways. Don't slant a verdict against the poor — and don't slant it for him just because he is poor. Truth, not sympathy, is the standard of judgment.

Truth, not sympathy, is the standard of judgment.

Raise no false report. Put not thy hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness. Follow not a multitude to do evil; speak not in a cause to wrest judgment. Neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause. Guidance: Fairness goes both ways. Don't slant a verdict against the poor — and don't slant it for him just because he is poor. Truth, not sympathy, is the standard of judgment.

If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again. If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, thou shalt surely help with him. Guidance: God's law refuses to let hate excuse cruelty. Your enemy's animal is still an animal in distress, and helping him reroutes the relationship itself.

If thou meet thine enemy's ox or his ass going astray, thou shalt surely bring it back to him again. If thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden, thou shalt surely help with him. Guidance: God's law refuses to let hate excuse cruelty. Your enemy's animal is still an animal in distress, and helping him reroutes the relationship itself.

Wrest not the judgment of thy poor in his cause. Keep far from a false matter; slay not the innocent, for I will not justify the wicked. Take no gift — for the gift blinds the wise and perverts the words of the righteous. Guidance: Bribes blind even the wise. Stay far from any deal that bends a verdict; God Himself will not call wickedness right.

Thou shalt not oppress a stranger — for ye know the heart of a stranger, seeing ye were strangers in the land of Egypt. Guidance: God's law makes empathy a memory exercise. You were strangers in Egypt; you know how it feels. Now don't do it to anyone else.

Six years thou shalt sow thy land; the seventh, let it rest, that the poor of thy people may eat — and what they leave, the beasts of the field. Do likewise with thy vineyard and oliveyard. Guidance: Sabbath is for land too. Every seventh year the field rests and the poor eat freely from what grows on its own. Even the soil belongs to God's economy of mercy.

Six years thou shalt sow thy land; the seventh, let it rest, that the poor of thy people may eat — and what they leave, the beasts of the field. Do likewise with thy vineyard and oliveyard. Guidance: Sabbath is for land too. Every seventh year the field rests and the poor eat freely from what grows on its own. Even the soil belongs to God's economy of mercy.

Six days thou shalt do thy work; the seventh day thou shalt rest — that thine ox and ass may rest, and the son of thy handmaid, and the stranger, may be refreshed. Guidance: Sabbath protects servant, foreigner, and animal — those who can't demand a day off. A weekly rest you take for yourself is incomplete; it must spill onto whoever serves you.

In all I have said to you be circumspect: make no mention of the name of other gods, neither let it be heard out of thy mouth. Guidance: What you name often, you befriend. Israel's worship begins with the speech she will not give to false gods. Watch your mouth as a guard for your heart.

Three times a year keep a feast unto Me. The feast of unleavened bread seven days in Abib — the month ye came out of Egypt; none shall appear before Me empty. The feast of harvest, firstfruits of thy labors. The feast of ingathering at year's end. All thy males shall appear before the Lord GOD. Guidance: Three pilgrimage feasts shape the year: Passover, Harvest, Ingathering — deliverance, firstfruits, completion. God's people order time by what He has done.

Thou shalt not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leaven; the fat of My feast shall not remain till morning. The first of the firstfruits of thy land bring into the house of the LORD. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother's milk. Guidance: Worship has shape. The strange-sounding kid-in-milk rule guards a creation boundary — don't blur life and the source of life into one dish.

Behold, I send an Angel before thee — to keep thee in the way, to bring thee to the place I have prepared. Obey His voice; provoke Him not; My name is in Him. If thou obey, I will be enemy to thine enemies. He shall go before thee and bring thee in. Guidance: The Angel carries God's own name. Many read Him as a pre-incarnate Son. The path forward is His to guard.

Bow not to their gods; utterly overthrow them and break their images. Serve the LORD your God; He shall bless thy bread and water, take sickness from thee. None shall cast their young nor be barren in thy land: I will fulfil the number of thy days. Guidance: God promises bread, water, health, fertility, full lifespan — but all hinges on undivided worship. Idols demand the very gifts God means to give freely.

I will send My fear before thee, destroying the peoples thou comest to. I will send hornets to drive out the Canaanite — not in one year, lest the land become desolate, but little by little till thou be increased. I will set thy bounds from the Red Sea to the sea of the Philistines, desert to river. Guidance: God's slow conquest is mercy. He waits for Israel to grow into the land so it doesn't return to wildness in their absence.

Thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor with their gods. They shall not dwell in thy land, lest they make thee sin against Me — for if thou serve their gods, it will surely be a snare unto thee. Guidance: Covenants travel both directions. A treaty with idolaters becomes a slow lesson in their gods. God draws a hard line because He knows how nearness shapes love.
